title: erigeron caespitosus var. grandiflorus
intro: Erigeron caespitosus var. grandiflorus, commonly known as the mountain fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Erigeron caespitosus var. grandiflorus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
